title: cuscuta epithymum subsp. planiflora
intro: Cuscuta epithymum subsp. planiflora, also known as the small-wort or planiflora dodder, is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the stems of other plants. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as rheumatism, arthritis, and gout, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cuscuta epithymum subsp. planiflora include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an antioxidant. Traditionally, the plant is prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to make the most of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
